name: Note for Approval
description: Formal NFA (Note for Approval / eNFA) following the Adani approval workflow format. Compact executive approval document with a structured header, summary, background, detailed justification, capability listing, benefits, and financial proposal. Used to seek management/board approval for CAPEX schemes.
system_instructions: You are a senior program manager writing a formal Note for Approval (NFA / eNFA) for an Adani group capital expenditure scheme. This document is presented to management and board-level approvers. Write in crisp, executive-level language — precise, factual, and direct. Avoid long paragraphs; prefer structured bullets and clear statements. Use Indian financial conventions: costs in Rs. Crs. (crores) with GST stated separately, e.g., '₹12.1 Cr + GST'. Quantify benefits wherever possible ('60% faster model deployment', '100% KPI standardization'). Each section must be grounded in facts from the source documents — do not invent figures. The tone must convey confidence and strategic clarity appropriate for a senior leadership approval forum.

sections.title: NFA Basic Details
sections.instructions:
Generate the NFA header/basic details as a two-column markdown pipe table. Each row is one field:

| Field | Details |
|-------|--------|
| NFA No | [Leave blank — auto-assigned] |
| Subject | [Full project title] |
| Location | [City / Organisation-wide] |
| Company | [Full legal entity name, e.g., Adani Energy Solutions Limited] |
| BU / Category | [Business unit code, e.g., AESL] |
| NFA Type | [e.g., Others-Others or Infrastructure] |
| NFA Sub Type | [e.g., Others-Others] |
| NFA Amount | [e.g., 12.1 Cr] |
| Department | [e.g., ENER Digital] |
| Project / Plant | [e.g., AESL / AEML-T / AEML-D] |

Extract all details from the source documents. Leave NFA No blank.

sections.title: NFA Summary
sections.instructions: Write the NFA Summary — a 3-5 sentence executive overview of the entire proposal. It must answer: (1) What is being approved? (platform/project name, purpose), (2) What problem does it solve? (current-state gap in one sentence), (3) What will it deliver? (key capabilities and outcomes), (4) How much and for how long? (total CAPEX amount and support/maintenance period). Use crisp, approval-note language. No bullet points — flowing sentences only. This is the first thing approvers read, so make every sentence count.

sections.title: NFA Background — Current Challenges
sections.instructions: Write the 'Current challenges' sub-section of NFA Background. Describe the current-state landscape and its specific pain points as a bullet list of 7-10 items. Each bullet should name a concrete problem: a fragmented system, a manual process, a data quality issue, a governance gap, or a scalability constraint. Be specific — name the actual systems, tools, or processes involved (e.g., SAP BW/BO, SQL Server, Power BI, Excel). Extract directly from source documents.

sections.title: NFA Background — Proposed Approach
sections.instructions: Write the 'Proposed Approach' sub-section of NFA Background. Describe the strategic plan and what will be built — 3-4 focused paragraphs covering: (1) the core platform migration and modernisation plan (what is being replaced and with what), (2) any additional data or analytics workloads being onboarded (e.g., Smart Meter data, AI/ML use cases), (3) operational or control tower capabilities enabled by the platform, (4) any agentic AI or advanced digital transformation capabilities that this platform enables in future. Write at a strategy level — not at implementation detail level.

sections.title: NFA Details
sections.instructions: Write the opening narrative of the NFA Details section. Explain in 2-3 concise paragraphs: (1) the strategic need for the platform — why a unified data foundation is essential now, (2) what the platform will specifically provide at a high level (architecture, governance, analytics, AI/ML readiness), (3) the intended scope boundary of this NFA (what is and is not included). This sets context before the capability and benefit details that follow.

sections.title: Platform / Solution Capabilities
sections.instructions: List the key capabilities the platform will deliver as a bullet list of 8-12 items. Each bullet should name one distinct capability and describe it in one sentence. Cover capabilities such as: unified data ingestion framework, lakehouse/medallion architecture, business-aligned data models and KPIs, data governance and stewardship, security and role-based access control, observability and usage analytics dashboards, BI and reporting capabilities (e.g., React + Power BI), MLOps framework. Extract from source documents — use the exact capability names mentioned.

sections.title: Scope Limitation and Future Phases
sections.instructions:
Write the Scope Limitation and Future Phases section with two labelled sub-sections:

**Scope of this NFA:** One paragraph clearly stating what this NFA covers — the platform deliverables, number of KPI dashboards to be validated, and support/maintenance period. Start with: 'The scope of this NFA is limited to...' Be explicit about what is included.

**Future Phases enabled by this platform:** A bullet list of 3-5 future initiatives that this platform will enable but are NOT part of this NFA. Examples: large-scale business use case development, Digital Control Tower, Agentic AI platform, large-scale AI model development. Each bullet should be one sentence.

sections.title: Benefits
sections.instructions: List the key benefits of the platform as a bullet list of 6-10 items. Quantify benefits wherever possible — use percentages, numbers, or comparative statements (e.g., '60% faster model deployment cycles', '100% Enterprise-wide KPI standardization'). Cover: operational efficiency gains (reduced manual effort, faster insights), data quality improvements, cost savings (decommissioning legacy platforms, reducing report duplication), AI/ML enablement, governance and auditability improvements, and scalability. Extract any metrics or quantified claims from the source documents.

sections.title: NFA Proposal
sections.instructions: Write the NFA Proposal — the formal financial approval request. This should be 2-3 concise sentences covering: (1) the approval being sought (action verb: 'Approval is sought to incur CAPEX amounting to...'), (2) the total amount including GST (e.g., '₹12.1 Cr + GST'), (3) the purpose (design, development, implementation, and vendor support for X years), and (4) if the cost is split across entities, state each entity's portion clearly (e.g., 'Of ₹12.1 Cr, Rs. X Cr is for [Entity A] and Rs. Y Cr for [Entity B]'). Extract exact figures from source documents.

sections.title: Approval Flow
sections.instructions:
Generate the Approval Flow table showing the roles in the approval chain. Output as a markdown pipe table:

| Role | Name |
|------|------|
| Initiator | [Name or blank] |
| Approver | [Name or blank] |
| Approver | [Name or blank] |
| Approver | [Name or blank] |

If names are available in the source documents, include them. If not, list the roles only and leave Name column blank. Typical roles: Initiator, Department Head, Finance Approver, Business Head, MD/CEO.
